description: Generic, evidence-cited deal-intelligence signals. Detects the deal SHAPE (expansion / program-aligned / consolidation), MOMENTUM (what makes this real and winnable now), and RISKS / landmines (what stalls or kills it) from linguistic shapes/themes only — never a company, product, speaker, or a specific transcript's wording. Every surfaced signal cites the customer's own sentence, so the output sharpens a seller's read without inventing anything. Logic lives in src/lib/deal-intel; this file is editable without code changes.

momentum_cues.label: Exec-sponsored resilience program to attach to
cues: resilience has the clearest senior attention

momentum_cues.label: Existing Cisco relationship opens the door

risk_cues.label: No single economic buyer — budgets are separate
cues: there may not be one person

risk_cues.label: Do NOT frame this as a SIEM competition
cues: there is no approved replacement project

risk_cues.label: Skills / operating-model constraint
cues: explain the results to the business

risk_cues.label: High credibility bar — evidence, not black-box

stakeholder_roles.label: Business champion
stakeholder_roles.play: Arm them with exec-legible business-risk framing and a tight next step they can carry internally.

stakeholder_roles.label: Cost / commercial gatekeeper
stakeholder_roles.play: Lead with cost governance — data tiering, retention, and what data belongs where — before any commitment.

stakeholder_roles.label: Security & risk owner
stakeholder_roles.play: Lead with access separation, data boundaries, and sovereignty; keep security and operations workflows distinct.

stakeholder_roles.label: Technical / feasibility evaluator
stakeholder_roles.play: Offer a bounded, credible test on one representative environment; show confidence and data gaps, not black-box answers.
